name: Right Humerus
description: Long bone of the upper arm. Articulates proximally with the glenoid cavity of the scapula and distally with the radius and ulna at the elbow.
articulations: Glenoid cavity of the scapula — glenohumeral (shoulder) joint
Head of the radius (via the capitulum) — elbow joint
Trochlear notch of the ulna (via the trochlea) — elbow joint
muscleAttachments: Deltoid — inserts at the deltoid tuberosity
Rotator cuff: supraspinatus, infraspinatus, teres minor — greater tubercle
Pectoralis major, teres major, latissimus dorsi — intertubercular groove
clinical: Surgical neck is a common fracture site; the axillary nerve and posterior circumflex humeral artery wrap around it.
Mid-shaft fractures can injure the radial nerve as it runs in the spiral groove.

landmarks.name: Head
landmarks.description: Rounded proximal articular surface that fits into the glenoid cavity of the scapula.

landmarks.name: Greater tubercle
landmarks.description: Lateral prominence; attachment for supraspinatus, infraspinatus, and teres minor.

landmarks.name: Lesser tubercle
landmarks.description: Anterior prominence; attachment site for the subscapularis muscle.

landmarks.name: Deltoid tuberosity
landmarks.description: Roughened V-shaped area on the mid-lateral shaft; insertion site of the deltoid muscle.

landmarks.name: Medial epicondyle
landmarks.description: Distal medial projection; common origin of the forearm flexor muscles. The ulnar nerve passes posterior to it.

landmarks.name: Lateral epicondyle
landmarks.description: Distal lateral projection; common origin of the forearm extensor muscles.

landmarks.name: Capitulum
landmarks.description: Rounded lateral articular surface that articulates with the head of the radius.

landmarks.name: Trochlea
landmarks.description: Spool-shaped medial articular surface that articulates with the trochlear notch of the ulna.
